etymology.description: 出(出) (chū, "go out; exit") and 現(現) (xiàn, "appear; present") together describe the act of coming into view or becoming visible.
etymology.interpretation: This word combines 出(出) (chū, "go out") with 現(現) (xiàn, "appear"), suggesting the action of emerging or manifesting into view, akin to 'coming out and becoming present'.

examples.chinese: 天空中出现了彩虹(天空中出現了彩虹)。
examples.english: A rainbow appeared in the sky.

examples.chinese: 问题又出现了(問題又出現了)，需要立即解决(需要立即解決)。
examples.pinyin: Wèntí yòu chūxiàn le, xūyào lìjí jiějué.
examples.english: The problem appeared again and needs to be resolved immediately.

examples.chinese: 每当他出现时(每當他出現時)，大家都会很高兴(大家都會很高興)。
examples.pinyin: Měidāng tā chūxiàn shí, dàjiā dōu huì hěn gāoxìng.
examples.english: Whenever he appears, everyone is very happy.
